chapter_title.en: HADEETH OF THE FUQAHA (JURISTS) AND THE ULAMA (SCHOLARS)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 486)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 486)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ (عليه السلام) يَقُولُ عَادَيْتُمْ فِينَا الْآبَاءَ وَ الْأَبْنَاءَ وَ الْأَزْوَاجَ وَ ثَوَابُكُمْ عَلَى اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَمَا إِنَّ أَحْوَجَ مَا تَكُونُونَ إِذَا بَلَغَتِ الْأَنْفُسُ إِلَى هَذِهِ وَ أَوْمَأَ بِيَدِهِ إِلَى حَلْقِهِ.

en.hubeali: H 14967 – Ahmad, from Ali Bin Al-Hakam, from Quteyba Al-A’ashy who said:

I heard Abu Abdullahasws saying: ‘You have antagonised your fathers, and your sons, and your wives with regards to usasws, and your Reward is upon Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic. But rather, it would be most needed when you are in a state when your souls reach to here’ – and heasws gestured by hisasws hand to hisasws throat’.
en.sarwar: 520. From him, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al-Hassan Bin Ali, from Dawood Bin Suleyman Al-Hammaar, from Saeed Bin Yasaar who said: We sought permission to see Abu Abdullah (a.s.), I, and Al-Haris Bin Al-Mugheira Al-Nasry, and Mansour Al-Sayaqal. So we agreed to meet at the house of Tahir, his (a.s.) slave. So we Prayed Al-Asr, then we went to him (a.s.). We found him (a.s.) leaning against a bed near to the ground. So we seated ourselves around him (a.s.). Then he (a.s.) sat upright and stretched his (a.s.) legs until he (a.s.) placed his (a.s.) feet upon the ground, then said: ‘The Praise is due to Allah (azwj) Who Made the people to go to the right and left as a Murjiite sect, and a Khwarijite sect, and a Qadiriyya sect, and you have been named as the Turabiyya’. Then said to the one seated on his (a.s.) right: ‘And Allah (azwj), there is no god except for Him (azwj), One with no associates to Him (azwj), and His (azwj) Messenger (saww), and the Progeny (a.s.) of His (azwj) Messenger (saww) and their (a.s.) Shites, may Allah (azwj) honour their faces, and he who was not upon that was not upon anything. By Allah (azwj), Ali (a.s.) was higher to the people than their own selves after the Rasool Allah (saww)’. He (a.s.) said it thrice’.
